summary: This week's AI news highlights significant advancements in various sectors, including the introduction of new hardware and models. OpenAI's GPT-Red and NVIDIA's Jetson Thor are notable examples. The industry is also seeing increased focus on AI safety, ethics, and governance.
insight: The development of AI models like GPT-Red, which can test and improve the robustness of other models, demonstrates the growing importance of AI safety and security in the industry.
highlights: OpenAI introduces GPT-Red, an LLM super-hacker to improve model safety
NVIDIA launches Jetson Thor for mainstream robotics and edge AI
Applied Computing raises $20M to build a foundation AI model for the oil and gas industry

articles.description: Applied Computing has raised a $20M Series A to build a foundation AI model for the oil, gas and petrochemical industry.

articles.description: Anthropic-backed Ode launches as AI labs bet that embedding forward-deployed engineers inside enterprises is the key to accelerating enterprise AI adoption.

articles.title: GPT-Red: Unlocking Self-Improvement for Robustness
articles.description: Explore GPT-Red, OpenAI’s automated red teaming system that uses self-play to improve AI safety, alignment, and prompt injection robustness.

articles.description: The funding discussions point to investor interest in applying AI to make breakthroughs in life sciences.

articles.description: OpenAI outlines a “reverse federalism” approach to AI governance, where state laws help build a national framework for safe, democratic AI.
